name: Ecom Bot — Slot Memory + Filtered Catalog

parameters.content:
## Ecom Bot — Slot Memory + Filtered Catalog + Anti-Hallucination Prompt

**One-time setup**
1. Credentials -> New -> "Header Auth". Name it "NocoDB Master Token", header name `xc-token`,
   value = your NocoDB personal access token (nc_pat_...). Never type the token into a node
   directly — every HTTP node below references this credential by id, not a literal value.
2. Open each HTTP Request node once and pick that credential from the dropdown (re-picking it
   after import re-links it to your own credential id — the id in this file is a placeholder).
3. Point the Webhook node's path at wherever you're forwarding Chatwoot's `message_created`
   event from (see SETUP.md "Channels module"), or wire this in as an Execute Workflow sub-flow
   called from your main bot engine — either works, nothing here assumes which.
4. `ecom_prefs` (LongText) must exist on the Leads table — see SETUP.md "Ecom bot memory".

See SETUP.md "Ecom bot memory (ecom_prefs) and product filtering" for the full schema and the
reasoning behind each rule in the prompt below.

parameters.jsCode:
const d = $input.first().json;
const prompt = `Extract shopping preferences mentioned in this WhatsApp message as STRICT JSON only — no prose, no markdown fences.

Fields (all optional — use null for anything not mentioned in THIS message):
- size (string)
- color (string)
- category (string)
- budget_max (number)
- language (ISO 639-1 code, e.g. "en", "ml", "hi" — detect from the message itself, don't guess from a name)

Message: "${d.messageText}"

Return ONLY a JSON object with exactly these five keys.`;

return [{ json: { ...d, extractionPrompt: prompt } }];

nodes.name: Code · Merge Slots + Resolve Products Table

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { model: $json.client.model || "google/gemini-2.5-flash", temperature: 0.4, messages: [{ role: "system", content: $json.systemPrompt }, { role: "user", content: $json.messageText }] } }}
={{ { Id: parseInt($('Code · Parse Incoming Message').first().json.leadId, 10), ecom_prefs: JSON.stringify($('Code · Extract Reply Text').first().json.merged) } }}

main.node: Code · Merge Slots + Resolve Products Table
